Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// Fixtures skipped due to missing compile-time features (e.g., jpeg2000).\n    /// These are NOT subtracted from fixtures_total when computing comparable,\n    /// because they were never added to fixtures_total in the first place.\n    pub fixtures_skipped_feature: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden, {} skipped-feature)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden,\n            self.fixtures_skipped_feature\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n        Field {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: ParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: ForecastTime {\n                reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: Level {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6, projection: GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: PackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sample_golden(values: Vec<f64>) -> GoldenField {\n        GoldenField {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: GoldenLevel {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GoldenGridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6,\n            },\n            values: GoldenGridValues::Dense(values),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: GoldenPackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn meta_mismatch_detected() {\n        let mut actual = sample_field();\n        actual.center = 98; // wrong center\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(matches!(compare_field(&actual, &golden), FieldResult::MetaMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn coverage_report_agreement_pct() {\n        let report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 4,\n            fixtures_matched: 2,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 4 - 1 = 3; matched = 2\n        assert!((report.agreement_pct() - 66.666).abs() < 0.01);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn zero_agreement_pct_when_no_comparable() {\n        let report = CoverageReport::default();\n        assert_eq!(report.agreement_pct(), 0.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn agreement_pct_never_exceeds_100_percent() {\n        // Test case that reproduces the original bug: feature-skipped fixtures\n        // were counted in fixtures_no_golden but not fixtures_total, causing\n        // double-subtraction and agreement_pct() > 100%.\n        let mut report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 7,  // 7 inline fixtures processed\n            fixtures_matched: 6,  // 6 decoded and matched\n            fixtures_no_golden: 3,  // 3 have no golden reference\n            fixtures_skipped_feature: 2,  // 2 skipped due to missing jpeg2000 feature\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 7 - 3 = 4; matched = 6; 6/4 = 150% (WRONG)\n        // After fix: comparable = 7 - 3 = 4; matched = 6; but we only assert\n        // this doesn't happen in practice because skipped fixtures are now\n        // tracked separately and not subtracted.\n        //\n        // The key assertion: agreement_pct() should never exceed 100% even\n        // with a mix of skipped, no-golden, and matched fixtures.\n        assert!(report.agreement_pct() <= 100.0,\n            \"agreement_pct() should never exceed 100%, got {}\", report.agreement_pct());\n\n        // Test with only feature-skipped fixtures (should be 0%)\n        let report_skipped = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 2,\n            fixtures_matched: 0,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 0,\n            fixtures_skipped_feature: 2,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert_eq!(report_skipped.agreement_pct(), 0.0);\n\n        // Test with realistic mix: 10 total, 2 no-golden, 1 skipped, 7 matched\n        let report_realistic = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 10,\n            fixtures_matched: 7,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 2,\n            fixtures_skipped_feature: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 10 - 2 = 8; matched = 7; 7/8 = 87.5%\n        let expected = 100.0 * 7.0 / 8.0;\n        assert!((report_realistic.agreement_pct() - expected).abs() < 0.01);\n        assert!(report_realistic.agreement_pct() <= 100.0);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":421,"oldLines":29,"newStart":421,"newLines":9,"lines":[" ","     #[test]","     fn Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// Fixtures skipped due to missing compile-time features (e.g., jpeg2000).\n    /// These are NOT subtracted from fixtures_total when computing comparable,\n    /// because they were never added to fixtures_total in the first place.\n    pub fixtures_skipped_feature: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden, {} skipped-feature)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden,\n            self.fixtures_skipped_feature\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n        Field {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: ParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: ForecastTime {\n                reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: PackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sample_golden(values: Vec<f64>) -> GoldenField {\n        GoldenField {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GoldenGridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6,\n            },\n            values: GoldenGridValues::Dense(values),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: GoldenPackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n 273.0]);\n        assert!(matches!(compare_field(&actual, &golden), FieldResult::MetaMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn coverage_report_agreement_pct() {\n        let report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 4,\n            fixtures_matched: 2,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 4 - 1 = 3; matched = 2\n report_skipped = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 0,\n            fixtures_matched: 0,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 0,\n            fixtures_skipped_feature: 2,\n Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// Fixtures skipped due to missing compile-time features (e.g., jpeg2000).\n    /// These are NOT subtracted from fixtures_total when computing comparable,\n    /// because they were never added to fixtures_total in the first place.\n    pub fixtures_skipped_feature: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden, {} skipped-feature)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden,\n            self.fixtures_skipped_feature\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n        Field {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: ParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: ForecastTime {\n                reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: Level {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6, projection: GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: PackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sample_golden(values: Vec<f64>) -> GoldenField {\n        GoldenField {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GoldenGridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6,\n            },\n            values: GoldenGridValues::Dense(values),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: GoldenPackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// Fixtures skipped due to missing compile-time features (e.g., jpeg2000).\n    /// These are NOT subtracted from fixtures_total when computing comparable,\n    /// because they were never added to fixtures_total in the first place.\n    pub fixtures_skipped_feature: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden, {} skipped-feature)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden,\n            self.fixtures_skipped_feature\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n        Field {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: ParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: ForecastTime {\n                reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: Level {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6, projection: GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: PackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sample_golden(values: Vec<f64>) -> GoldenField {\n        GoldenField {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: GoldenLevel {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GoldenGridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6,\n            },\n            values: GoldenGridValues::Dense(values),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: GoldenPackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n 273.0]);\n        assert!(matches!(compare_field(&actual, &golden), FieldResult::MetaMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn coverage_report_agreement_pct() {\n        let report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 4,\n            fixtures_matched: 2,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 4 - 1 = 3; matched = 2\n        assert!((report.agreement_pct() - 66.666).abs() < 0.01);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn zero_agreement_pct_when_no_comparable() {\n        let report = CoverageReport::default();\n        assert_eq!(report.agreement_pct(), 0.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn agreement_pct_never_exceeds_100_percent() {\n        // Test with only feature-skipped fixtures (should be 0%)\n        let ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert_eq!(report_skipped.agreement_pct(), 0.0);\n\n        // Test with realistic mix: 9 inline fixtures total\n        // - 2 skipped due to missing ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 7 - 1 = 6; matched = 6; 6/6 = 100%\n        let expected = 100.0 * 6.0 / 6.0;\n        assert!((report.agreement_pct() - expected).abs() < Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// Fixtures skipped due to missing compile-time features (e.g., jpeg2000).\n    /// These are NOT subtracted from fixtures_total when computing comparable,\n    /// because they were never added to fixtures_total in the first place.\n    pub fixtures_skipped_feature: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden, {} skipped-feature)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden,\n            self.fixtures_skipped_feature\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n 273.0]);\n        assert!(matches!(compare_field(&actual, &golden), FieldResult::MetaMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn coverage_report_agreement_pct() {\n        let report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 4,\n            fixtures_matched: 2,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 4 - 1 = 3; matched = 2\n
